text:
Member-only story

# Second Thoughts: Improving Small LLMs with Bidirectional Refinement Loops. Part 1.

I was wondering about small models, hallucinations, and other problems the other day, and was immediately reminded of a quote from The Tiffany Aching series:

“First Thoughts are the everyday thoughts. Everyone has those. Second Thoughts are the thoughts you think about the way you think. People who enjoy thinking have those. Third Thoughts are thoughts that watch the world and think all by themselves. They’re rare, and often troublesome. Listening to them is part of witchcraft.”


― Tiffany Aching,[A Hat Full of Sky]by Terry Pratchett

**First Thoughts**

So an LLM is one and done. It has attention, it has cached bits of memory, but things flow through in a single direction. Every model works this way.

<think> tags have been a major boost/bane for LLMs. The idea was simply that the model would take some time to work through the problem before it output it’s “final answer.” This has led to situations where thinking never ends, or it hallucinates the solutions/answers. “But wait…” loops can burn through a lot of tokens before a final, incorrect, output can happen.

Essentially, you’re forcing Attention and KV to attend to many more tokens related to your problem. Statistically, this…
